name: Bhai Amar Singh (Delhi)
body: Bhai Amar Singh Ji was born in 1938 in the city of Jhang Maghiana of undivided Punjab which is now in Pakistan. Both his father and older brother used to perform Kirtan. Besides their passion for Kirtan, they also manufactured kitchen utensils for their livelihood. Both of them used to perform Kirtan in Nanaksar during the times of Baba Nand Singh Ji and Baba Isher Singh Ji. Bhai Amar Singh Ji used to accompany them but he was very young at that time. His journey into music was initiated by his father from whom he learned Kirtan. Following the partition of Punjab in 1947, they moved to Jagadhri, Haryana. After settling in Jagadhri, Bhai Amar Singh Ji furthered his training in Shastri Sangeet under the guidance of Pandit Khoob Chand Ji, who belonged to the Gwalior Gharana. Pandit Khoob Chand Ji was trained by Madhav Moreshvar Kale, who was better known as Balasaheb Guruji in Gwalior. Balasaheb Guruji was a disciple of Ustad Haddu Khan of the Gwalior Gharana.

Bhai Amar Singh Ji was then brought to Delhi by Sant Sujan Singh Ji’s nephew, Prof. Kartar Singh Ji. He stayed with Sant Sujan Singh Ji for many years and continued to learn Kirtan from him. He was taught many Raags and old compositions. He accompanied Bhai Angad Singh Ji on the side harmonium for 7 years before forming his own jatha sometime between 1966-1968. He continued to live in Delhi and performed Kirtan with his jatha in various historical Gurdwaras in Delhi such as Gurdwara Bangla Sahib and Gurdwara Sees Ganj Sahib. Some of his well known ‘saathis’ include Bhai Hardev Singh from Amritsar and Bhai Santokh Singh Thawar. From 1997 onward, his only son, Bhai Amarjit Singh Raja joined him until his retirement in 2005. Bhai Amar Singh Ji made approximately 25 tours abroad to various countries including USA, Canada, Singapore, Malaysia, Iran, Australia and many more. It was his wish to perform Kirtan to the very end and in some ways that is what happened. Bhai Amar Singh Ji had just returned home after Kirtan one day and he sat down on his sofa. Shortly after sitting down, he suffered a stroke and became paraylsed. He then slipped into a coma for some time and passed away in 2007.
A special thanks to Bhai Amarjit Singh Raja for providing the information above. If anyone has more recordings of Bhai Amar Singh Ji, please contact us.
